response.justification: Maria has a genuinely interesting geospatial background through GISCollective and Green Map, and some distributed systems exposure from her time at Sociomantic, but the role's core requirement — modern C++ or Rust — is essentially absent from her current skillset. Her one C/C++ role was a six-month stint in 2014–2015, and C++ does not even appear in her skills section. Her entire recent trajectory (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, AI tooling) points decisively away from systems-level compiled languages and routing pipeline work.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS experience via GISCollective and Green Map, including a FOSS4G conference talk — directly adjacent to the problem domain
Proven ability to build and operate high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second with async I/O)
Strong track record of polyglot adaptability and full ownership across the stack, suggesting ability to ramp on new technical areas
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade stale — a single six-month role at NI in 2014–2015, and C++ is absent from the skills list entirely
No Rust experience mentioned anywhere in the resume
No exposure to routing data pipelines, navigation tile generation, map data transformation, or anything resembling the core pipeline engineering work described
